assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been recognised as a receivable and gives rise to a foreign-currency-denominated monetary item.
The question concerns the accounting in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge accounting under IFRS 9
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The question explicitly asks about eligibility for designation in hedge accounting, and the context includes IFRS 9 requirements for hedged items and foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency accounting under IAS 21
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The issue concerns exchange differences on an intragroup receivable in consolidated accounts, and IAS 21 in the context addresses recognition of exchange differences on monetary items and intragroup balances.

approaches.label: Dividend recognition accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Because the fact pattern refers to a dividend receivable already recognised, the accounting model for when dividends are recognised is directly relevant to the existence of the underlying item.
